steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question focused on emphasis. The notes give two specific measurements (4.46 cm for Gygis alba, 7.22 cm for Gavia stellata). The correct choice must use both measurements and explicit contrast language to make the size difference impossible to miss.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘A 2017 study compared the lengths of eggs produced by an array of different bird species, such as Gygis alba and Gavia stellata.’: Names both species but omits both measurements entirely. Without numbers, no difference is emphasized. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘A 2017 study found that Gygis alba eggs had an average length of 4.46 cm, whereas Gavia stellata eggs were longer, with an average length of 7.22 cm.’: Includes both measurements, the contrast word “whereas,” and the explicit phrase “were longer” — all three together make the difference unmissable. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘The bird species Gygis alba … and Gavia stellata … were included in a 2017 study that compared the average lengths of their eggs.’: Notes that egg lengths were compared but provides neither measurement nor any language emphasizing a difference. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Mary Caswell Stoddard led a research study that determined the average lengths of eggs, including those of Gygis alba birds (4.46 cm) and Gavia stellata birds (7.22 cm).’: Lists both measurements in parentheses but uses no contrast language (“whereas,” “longer,” etc.), so the difference is merely implied, not emphasized. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
